summary: type-context ships pino-lookalike that spawns detached child to fetch and execute remote JavaScript
details: The package presents itself as a pino-style logger (keywords fast/logger/stream/json, module.exports.pino = middleware, internal require('./pino')) but on first use of the exported middleware factory in index.js it spawns a detached `node lib/caller.js` child process with stdio ignored. caller.js performs an HTTPS GET to https://json.extendsclass.com/bin/250fca079abb and executes the returned `.cookie` field as JavaScript via `new Function.constructor('require', s)(require)`, giving the remote content full Node/require capabilities on the installer's host. Destination endpoints are obscured: caller.js and lib/const.js define a shadow `process` object with base64-encoded DEV_API_KEY values that decode to jsonkeeper.com URLs (e.g. aHR0cHM6Ly9qc29ua2VlcGVyLmNvbS9iL1hSR0Yz -> https://jsonkeeper.com/b/XRGF3). The remote content is hosted on mutable third-party JSON-bin services, is unpinned and unverified, and can be swapped at any time by whoever controls those bins.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
